mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage floods the edge map with (call-context, edge) pairs inside the high-call-count inflate loop, diverting mutation energy from byte changes that would produce the leftover-data condition.
one_line: ctx coverage dilutes inflate-loop edges, starving the extra-data condition

mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage splits scan-loop edges across many call-context slots, fragmenting the corpus and preventing assembly of the long CR/LF char-data run that plain havoc builds naturally.
one_line: ctx coverage fragments loop edges, blocking the CR/LF char-data run

mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage dilutes the edge-map signal that would reinforce the xMl name byte across call contexts, so corpus fills with context-duplicate seeds that never carry the m/M byte at name[1].
one_line: ctx coverage dilutes xMl name-byte signal across call-context splits
